regime_read: ES & NQ moving together — broad agreement, more DURABLE directional signal. Trust the direction more.
narrative:
Macro backdrop —
[2026-06-25] US economic state (FRED):
  Growth: real GDP 1.6% (q/q ann)
  Inflation: CPI 3.9% YoY | core CPI 2.58% | core PCE 2.78% (Fed target 2%)
  Labor: unemployment 4.3%
  Rates: fed funds 3.63% | 10Y 4.5% | 10Y-2Y 0.3% (NORMAL)
  Consumer: UMich sentiment 49.8 | VIX 19.49

---
PRE-US-OPEN BRIEFING — ES/NQ — as of 2026-06-25 ~12:26 UTC

1) RECENT MOVE-BY-MOVE READ
Both indices are firm on the day, with NQ leading the strength: NQ +2.13% daily vs ES +0.65%. NQ is trading right at its prior-day high (last 30,143.75 vs PDH 30,157.75) after a wide prior-day range (29,264.75–30,157.75), which signals a strong recovery bounce off the lows back toward the top of yesterday's session. ES sits at 7,476.5, mid-range against its PDH 7,496.5 / PDL 7,404.25, and effectively pinned to its session VWAP (7,479.19) — balance, not breakout. The likely catalysts are macro/geopolitical: the dominant news thread is de-escalation in the Middle East — Trump offering Iran an 'economic lifeline,' Rubio/UAE discussing an Iran deal, and an associated oil slide. A softening geopolitical risk premium plus lower oil is a classic risk-on tailwind, and it's showing up more in tech-heavy NQ. There is no clean, single index-moving tech earnings catalyst in the tagged headlines (Qualcomm/ByteDance chip talk is constructive for semis but not a tape-mover here). Net read: a relief/risk-on grind, NQ-led, but neither index has cleared the prior day's high decisively.

2) VOLUME-PROFILE READ (volume-at-price, NOT order-flow delta — respect that)
This is WHERE volume traded over 5 days, not buy/sell aggression. ES: POC 7,541.61 sits ABOVE current price, with value area 7,416.85–7,546.23. Price (7,476.5) is in the lower-middle of value, below POC — meaning the heavy-volume shelf overhead (7,541) is the magnet/resistance and the line in the sand on the downside is the value-area low ~7,417 (which also aligns with PDL ~7,404). NQ: POC 29,729.43 sits BELOW current price, with value area 29,442–30,633. NQ has pushed to the upper end of its value area and is now trading above its POC — a more constructive structure than ES. The accumulation zone for NQ is the 29,688–29,852 HVN cluster; the line in the sand on the downside is value-area low ~29,442, then the prior-week/prior-day low pivot at 29,264.75. Upside, the 30,633 VAH then prior-week high 30,968 are the next references. Slight asymmetry: NQ structurally above POC (bullish lean) while ES is below POC (still working back up to its volume shelf) — consistent with NQ leading.

3) NQ/ES LEAD-LAG REGIME — BREADTH
Contemporaneous correlation is high at 0.942, lead-lag is essentially noise (best lag corr only 0.098, leader = NONE), and divergence is FALSE. The system classifies this as BREADTH: ES and NQ are moving together, which makes the directional signal more DURABLE and more trustworthy. Caveat: the 1-day return spread (NQ over ES) is elevated (+1.48%) versus the 5d/20d averages (~0.18%/0.07%), so today's NQ outperformance is a short-term stretch, not a 20-day trend. Implication: trust the direction more, but do not over-size on NQ's single-day burst — the broad agreement supports a directional lean, while the stretched 1d spread argues against chasing NQ extension here. Note: this is a regime INPUT, not an edge, and there's no execution path in this pair (CME futures; research only).

4) EVENT-CALENDAR AWARENESS
Dense macro runway ahead, all cadence ESTIMATES (not verified): PCE in ~5d (2026-06-30, 8:30 ET — Fed's preferred inflation, reduce size into the window); ISM Manufacturing in ~6d (07-01, 10:00 ET); Nonfarm Payrolls in ~8d (07-03, 8:30 ET — high impact, widen stops / size down); CPI in ~17d (07-12). No event hits TODAY's open, so intraday positioning is unconstrained by the calendar — but plan to throttle size into the PCE window late next week. Treat all these dates as approximate.

5) SYNTHESIS — META-REGIME + NARROWED BIAS
Meta-regime: risk-on relief grind driven by geopolitical de-escalation + falling oil, expressed through tech (NQ-led), inside a clean BREADTH correlation regime (corr 0.94, no divergence). Both indices remain well above the 200-day (ES +7.6%, NQ +15.6%), so the larger trend is intact; RSI is neutral (ES 44, NQ 48), leaving room in either direction. The constructive pieces: NQ reclaiming above its POC and pressing PDH, broad agreement that makes the up-lean more durable. The cautionary pieces: NQ's 1d outperformance is stretched vs its own average, ES is still below its POC and pinned to VWAP (balance), and the bullish catalyst is geopolitical/headline-driven (reversal-prone). Net: a LONG-leaning bias, but a measured one — long-the-pullback rather than chase-the-breakout. BIAS: LONG, ~58%, invalidated below 29,264.75 on NQ (prior-day / prior-week low pivot; a break there also takes price out of the NQ value area and flips the structure). A failure of NQ to hold above its POC (~29,729) with ES rejecting back below VWAP would be the early warning that the relief move is stalling. CONFIDENCE GRADE: moderate (58). Lead-lag is clean (supports higher confidence), but news is broadly risk-on rather than a single clear hard catalyst, and the NQ move is short-term stretched (caps confidence). This is INTELLIGENCE, not a validated edge — the briefing scorecard (n=7, regime accuracy 0.375) is still CALIBRATING and not yet statistically meaningful; size accordingly.
